title: Academy of Management Review
notes: Purely conceptual/theoretical — but verbal theory, not formal models.

title: Econometrica
notes: Premier theory journal. Mechanism design, game theory, decision theory.

title: American Economic Review
notes: Publishes major theory contributions; needs broad appeal.

title: American Journal of Political Science
notes: Has formal theory section. Political economy, regulation.

title: American Political Science Review
notes: Formal political economy models. Voting, institutions, information.

title: Journal of Finance
notes: Asset pricing theory, market microstructure theory.

title: Journal of Financial Economics
notes: Corporate finance theory, contracting models.

title: Journal of Political Economy
notes: Political economy theory, information economics.

title: Management Science
notes: Formal models in OR, mechanism design, platform economics, decision theory.

title: Marketing Science
notes: Game-theoretic models of platforms, information, pricing.

title: Operations Research
notes: Game-theoretic/mechanism design models if framed as OR.

title: Review of Economic Studies
notes: ~30-40% theory. Strong tradition.

title: Review of Financial Studies
notes: Financial theory. Less relevant for your topics.

title: Information Systems Research
notes: Occasionally publishes formal models (mechanism design for platforms, information markets).

title: MIS Quarterly: Management Information Systems
notes: Design science and formal models occasionally. Predominantly empirical.

title: Organization Science
notes: Formal/computational organisation theory (rare but exists). NK models, agent-based.

title: Quarterly Journal of Economics
notes: Strongly prefers empirical. Hardest top-5 for pure theory.

title: Research Policy
notes: Policy-relevant theoretical frameworks. More conceptual than formal.

title: Strategic Management Journal
notes: Game-theoretic strategy models exist but rare. Predominantly empirical.

title: Journal of Economic Theory
notes: Dedicated theory journal — entire mandate.

title: Mathematical Programming
notes: Optimisation theory. Accepts mechanism design with algorithmic flavour.

title: Psychological Review
notes: Formal models of cognition, judgment, decision-making.

title: RAND Journal of Economics
notes: IO theory, regulation, mechanism design.

title: Theoretical Economics
notes: Econometric Society theory journal. Open access.

title: Economic Journal
notes: Generalist; accepts theory with policy relevance.

title: European Journal of Operational Research
notes: Decision theory, game theory, formal MCDM models.

title: International Economic Review
notes: Micro theory, game theory, trade theory.

title: International Organization
notes: Formal models of international regulatory competition, governance.

title: Journal of International Economics
notes: Trade theory models. Narrower scope.

title: Journal of Monetary Economics
notes: Macro/monetary theory models (DSGE, policy).

title: Journal of Politics
notes: Formal models of institutions, information, regulation.

title: Journal of the European Economic Association
notes: Theory with European/policy angle.

title: Quantitative Economics
notes: Econometric Society journal; theoretical econometrics and economic theory.

title: Governance
notes: Regulatory governance theory. Compliance/enforcement framing.

title: Journal of Management Studies
notes: Methods SI accepts formal/analytical papers. Otherwise empirical.

title: Journal of Public Administration Research and Theory
notes: Accepts formal models of regulation/public admin.

title: Nature Human Behaviour
notes: Accepts theoretical/computational models with strong behavioural framing.

title: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes
notes: Formal decision models if behaviourally grounded.

title: Socio-Economic Review
notes: Institutional/comparative; accepts analytical frameworks.
